title: Joker
logline: A mentally ill party clown in 1981 Gotham descends into violent madness after society abandons him, discovering his identity through chaos and becoming an accidental symbol for the city's disenfranchised.
antagonistOrConflict: Society's systemic cruelty and indifference; Thomas Wayne as personal antagonist; Arthur's own fractured psyche
genre: Psychological thriller / Character study / Crime drama
tone: Gritty, intimate, darkly comedic, tragic, increasingly unhinged
themes: The construction of identity through delusion
Violence as response to systemic cruelty
The thin line between comedy and tragedy

actBreaks.description: Arthur loses his social worker and medication support as the city cuts funding, severing his last institutional lifeline and pushing him toward isolation.

validation.ruling: Scene #43 shows Joker learning his social services are being cut—a setback but not an irreversible turning point. Scene #37 (p33, 27%) ends Act 1: Joker kills three men on the subway, a major irreversible action that fundamentally changes the story's direction and forces him into new, higher-stakes conflict as a fugitive and accidental symbol.

actBreaks.description: At his mother's grave, Arthur fully rejects his past identity and the detectives' attempt to bring him back, committing to his transformation into Joker.

validation.ruling: Scene #88 shows detectives confronting Joker at the cemetery—an important scene but occurs after the act break. Scene #85 (p88, 73%) ends Act 2: the camera pulls back from behind the curtain as Joker finishes with his hospitalized mother (strongly implied he smothered her), a dark irreversible choice that completes his transformation and propels him toward the climactic TV appearance.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This script operates as a slow-burn psychological descent structured around systematic stripping away. Act One methodically establishes Arthur's fragile ecosystem—his job, his mother, his medication, his fantasies—while planting seeds of violence (the subway killing at scene 37). Act Two becomes a brutal dismantling: he loses his job (scene 52-53), discovers his mother's lies (scenes 76-80), and has his romantic delusions shattered (scene 81). The structure is relentlessly linear, tracking Arthur's transformation through accumulating humiliations until he crosses the point of no return. Act Three (post-scene 88) is pure apotheosis—Arthur fully inhabits Joker, orchestrates his Murray Franklin appearance, and becomes the symbol he always fantasized about being. The script's genius lies in how it makes Arthur's violence feel inevitable through structural compression: each act tightens the noose until explosion becomes the only release.

The framing device (scenes 1 and 127-128) creates a circular quality within the linear structure, suggesting everything we've watched may be Arthur's unreliable narration to a psychiatrist. This adds a layer of ambiguity without disrupting the forward momentum of his transformation.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The first act moves deliberately, establishing routine through repetition (scenes 4-10, 17-25 mirror each other). Pacing accelerates dramatically after the subway murders (scene 37), with scenes getting shorter and more fragmented. The middle section (scenes 43-88) maintains relentless pressure through rapid location changes and accumulating revelations. The final act (scenes 89-126) becomes almost breathless—22 scenes in 32 pages—as Arthur prepares for and executes his Murray Franklin appearance. The script uses extended dialogue scenes (the Murray interview spans scenes 106-121) to create unbearable tension before explosive violence. Notable pacing shift: scenes 95-101 play almost in real-time as Arthur flees the detectives, creating visceral urgency before the climactic studio sequence.

incitingIncident.description: Three drunk Wall Street guys harass a young woman on the subway, then notice Joker laughing uncontrollably and mock him; when they approach him aggressively, Joker shoots all three of them.

validation.ruling: Scene #2 shows Joker working as a clown, getting harassed by kids—this is his ordinary world, not its shattering. Scene #37 (p33, 27%) is the true inciting incident: Joker kills three men on the subway in self-defense, an irreversible act that establishes the main conflict, introduces life-or-death stakes, and forces him into a new reality he cannot escape from.

midpoint.description: Joker walks along the fence of Wayne Manor and uses a magic wand to perform tricks for an eight-year-old boy hiding in the trees, making the boy laugh before an older woman pulls the child away and Joker is left alone at the gate.
midpoint.sceneHeading: 56 EXT. WAYNE MANOR, FRONT LAWN - MAGIC HOUR 56 56

validation.ruling: Scene #56 shows Joker meeting young Bruce Wayne at the gate—a character moment but not a structural pivot. Scene #71 (p73, 60%) is the true midpoint: Joker confronts Thomas Wayne in the bathroom and learns his mother lied about their relationship and his adoption, a devastating revelation that shifts him from seeking validation to embracing his darker identity—a clear point of no return with raised personal stakes.

climax.description: Joker tells Murray he's awful for inviting him on just to make fun of him, and when Murray defends himself, Joker pulls out a revolver and shoots Murray in the head on live television.
climax.sceneHeading: 119 INT. TALK SHOW SET, STAGE - STUDIO 4B - CONTINUOUS 119 119

validation.ruling: Scene #119 is the climax: Joker shoots Murray Franklin on live television at 94% through the script, the peak confrontation that resolves the main story question, demonstrates his complete transformation, and triggers the final consequences—this is the ultimate act, not setup.

scenes.heading: 1 INT. DEPT. OF HEALTH, OFFICE - MORNING 1 1
scenes.summary: Joker sits in a social worker's cramped office, recovering from a laughing fit, and discusses the tense state of Gotham and his job while she waits patiently.

scenes.heading: 2 EXT. GOTHAM SQUARE, MIDTOWN - KENNY'S MUSIC SHOP - DAY 2 2
scenes.summary: Joker works as a hobo clown in Gotham Square holding a sign for Kenny's Music Shop when five boys steal his sign and run off, prompting him to chase after them.

scenes.heading: 3 EXT. CORNER, SIDE STREET - GOTHAM SQUARE - CONTINUOUS 3 3
scenes.summary: Joker chases the boys down a side street where one ambushes him with the sign, knocking him down, and the group beats him while he's on the ground.

scenes.heading: 4 INT. CITY BUS (PULLING OUT) - HEADING DOWNTOWN - DUSK 4 4
scenes.summary: Joker rides the bus home with his makeup washed off and makes faces at a sad four-year-old girl until her angry mother yells at him to leave her daughter alone.

scenes.heading: 5 EXT. LOWER EAST SIDE, STREETS - GOTHAM - EVENING 5 5
scenes.summary: Joker walks through the poor, garbage-filled streets of the Lower East Side and enters a run-down drug store while two drunks fight on the corner behind him.

scenes.heading: 6 INT. LOBBY, APARTMENT BUILDING - EVENING 6 6
scenes.summary: Joker checks his mailbox in the shabby apartment building lobby and finds it empty.

scenes.heading: 7 INT. ELEVATOR, APARTMENT BUILDING - CONTINUOUS 7 7
scenes.summary: Joker holds the elevator door open for Sophie and her daughter Gigi, who thank him for his courtesy, and they ride up together in awkward silence.

scenes.heading: 8 INT. MOM'S APARTMENT, FRONT DOOR - EVENING 8 8
scenes.summary: Joker enters his mother's apartment, leans against the door swooning over Sophie, sees an old orange cat, then tells his mother there was no letter in the mail.

scenes.heading: 9 INT. MOM'S BEDROOM, APARTMENT - NIGHT 9 9
scenes.summary: Joker brings dinner to his bedridden mother Penny, who complains that Thomas Wayne hasn't responded to her letters despite her twelve years working for his family, while the news covers Wayne's mayoral campaign.

scenes.heading: 10 INT. LIVING ROOM, MOM'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 10 10
scenes.summary: Late at night, Joker writes in his joke notebook: "The worst part about having a mental illness is that people still expect you to behave as if you don't."

scenes.heading: 11 EXT. WAYNE TOWER, STREET - MIDTOWN - MORNING 11 11
scenes.summary: Joker stands outside Wayne Tower holding his mother's letter, looking small and out of place among the professionals, then heads inside.

scenes.heading: 12 INT. RECEPTION, WAYNE ENTERPRISES - 25TH FLOOR - MORNING 12 12
scenes.summary: Joker tries to deliver his mother's letter directly to Thomas Wayne at the reception desk, explaining she was the Wayne family housekeeper, but the receptionist refuses and security is called.

scenes.heading: 13 EXT. WAYNE TOWER, FRONT ENTRANCE - MORNING 13 13
scenes.summary: Two security guards forcibly throw Joker out onto the street, and when he tries to run back inside, they stiff-arm him away.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. HA-HA'S TALENT BOOKING, LOCKER ROOM - DAY 14 14
scenes.summary: In the locker room, Randall notices Joker's bruises from the beating and gives him a gun for protection, which Joker initially refuses before Randall insists he take it.
scenes.turningPointNote: Randall gives Joker the gun that will later be used in the subway killings — a pivotal object entering the story.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. FRONT OFFICE, HA-HA'S TALENT BOOKING - DAY 15 15
scenes.summary: Hoyt fires Joker after learning he brought a gun into the children's hospital ward, accusing him of trying to buy it from Randall and forcing him to say "I'm a fuck up" before hanging up.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joker loses his job, a major destabilizing event that removes structure from his life and increases his isolation.

scenes.heading: 16 EXT. BACK ALLEY, OUTSIDE HA-HA'S - AMUSEMENT MILE - DAY 16 16
scenes.summary: From the far end of an alley, we see Joker violently kicking and stomping on something behind a dumpster, though we cannot make out what it is.

scenes.heading: 17 INT. CITY BUS (MOVING) - DUSK 17 17
scenes.summary: Joker sits on the bus at the end of his work day, watching a young couple playfully teasing each other across the aisle.

scenes.heading: 18 EXT. GOTHAM, LOWER EAST SIDE - EVENING 18 18
scenes.summary: Joker walks home through the garbage-filled streets carrying the paper bag Randall gave him.

scenes.heading: 19 INT. LOBBY, APARTMENT BUILDING - EVENING 19 19
scenes.summary: Joker checks his mailbox in the lobby and finds it empty.

scenes.heading: 20 INT. ELEVATOR, APARTMENT BUILDING - CONTINUOUS 20 20
scenes.summary: Joker repeatedly holds the elevator door open with his foot, waiting and hoping Sophie will arrive, but no one comes.

scenes.heading: 21 INT. MOM'S APARTMENT, BATHROOM - NIGHT 21 21
scenes.summary: Joker gives his mother a bath, shampooing her hair carefully, while she asks about the letter and he lies that they promised to deliver it to Thomas Wayne.

scenes.heading: 22 INT. MOM'S BEDROOM, APARTMENT - LATE NIGHT 22 22
scenes.summary: Joker's mother lies unconscious in her bedroom with a half-eaten plate of food beside her.

scenes.heading: 23 INT. LIVING ROOM, MOM'S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS 23 23
scenes.summary: Joker sits on the couch holding the revolver Randall gave him, pointing it at the TV, the cat, and his own head before accidentally firing a shot into the wall.

scenes.heading: 24 INT. MOM'S BEDROOM, DOORWAY - CONTINUOUS 24 24
scenes.summary: Joker checks on his mother after the gunshot, tells her the noise was from a war movie on TV, and promises to turn it down.

scenes.heading: 25 INT. KITCHEN, MOM'S APARTMENT - LATE NIGHT 25 25
scenes.summary: Joker writes in his journal about Randall not telling him the gun was loaded, crossing out "could have killed myself" and changing it to "should kill myself."

scenes.heading: 26 EXT. STREET, LOWER EAST SIDE - MORNING 26 26
scenes.summary: Joker watches his own building from across the street with his hood up, waiting until Sophie exits with her daughter, then begins following them.

scenes.heading: 27 EXT. PUBLIC SCHOOL - MORNING 27 27
scenes.summary: Joker follows Sophie as she drops her daughter GiGi off at school.

scenes.heading: 28 EXT. ELEVATED SUBWAY PLATFORM - MORNING 28 28
scenes.summary: Joker hides behind a steel beam on the subway platform, watching Sophie from a distance as she waits and smokes a cigarette.

scenes.heading: 29 INT. SUBWAY (MOVING) - MORNING 29 29
scenes.summary: Joker stands between subway cars watching Sophie read a book, then exits when she does.

scenes.heading: 30 EXT. STREET, UPPER EAST SIDE - MORNING 30 30
scenes.summary: Joker follows Sophie to Gotham First National Bank in a nicer part of town and watches her enter for work.

scenes.heading: 31 INT. GOTHAM FIRST NATIONAL - LATER 31 31
scenes.summary: Joker approaches Sophie's teller window pretending he didn't know she worked there, tells her he's a stand-up comedian, and awkwardly asks her on a date which she accepts.

scenes.heading: 32 INT. GOTHAM COMEDY CLUB, CHINATOWN - NIGHT 32 32
scenes.summary: Joker sits alone in a crowded comedy club watching a successful comic perform, studying the act and taking notes in his notebook while everyone else laughs.

scenes.heading: 33 EXT. GOTHAM COMEDY CLUB, STREET - CHINATOWN - NIGHT 33 33
scenes.summary: Joker exits the club, sees a flyer advertising open mic night on Thursdays, and rips it off the wall.

scenes.heading: 34 INT. HA-HA'S TALENT BOOKING, LOCKER ROOM - DAY 34 34
scenes.summary: Joker applies his clown makeup in the locker room, then examines the scars at the corners of his mouth, pulling them down into a frown and releasing them back into a smile repeatedly.

scenes.heading: 35 INT. GOTHAM GENERAL - CHILDREN'S WARD - EVENING 35 35
scenes.summary: Joker performs as Mr. Jingles for sick children in a hospital ward, playing ukulele and leading them in singing "If You're Happy and You Know It."

scenes.heading: 36 INT. LOBBY, GOTHAM GENERAL - LATER 36 36
scenes.summary: Hoyt fires Joker over the phone after learning he brought a gun into the children's ward, revealing that Randall told him about it, and forces Joker to admit he's a liar before hanging up.

scenes.heading: 37 INT. SUBWAY (MOVING) - NIGHT 37 37
scenes.summary: Three drunk Wall Street guys harass a young woman on the subway, then notice Joker laughing uncontrollably and mock him; when they approach him aggressively, Joker shoots all three of them.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joker commits his first murders, killing three Wall Street employees — the irreversible act that transforms him and ignites the film's central conflict.

scenes.heading: 38 EXT. SUBWAY PLATFORM - CONTINUOUS 38 38
scenes.summary: Joker chases the third Wall Street guy onto the empty subway platform and shoots him as he runs toward the stairs, then stands over the body and fires three more shots.

scenes.heading: 39 EXT. STREET, ROBINSON SQUARE PARK - NIGHT 39 39
scenes.summary: Joker runs out of the subway station across a busy street and disappears into a dark, run-down park.

scenes.heading: 40 INT. PUBLIC BATHROOM, ROBINSON SQUARE PARK - NIGHT 40 40
scenes.summary: Joker locks himself in a public bathroom and vomits, then sees himself in the mirror holding the gun, points it at his head, pulls the trigger, but it only clicks empty.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. APARTMENT BUILDING, LOBBY - LATER 41 41
scenes.summary: Joker rushes into his apartment building and races up the stairs two at a time.

scenes.heading: 42 INT. HALLWAY, APARTMENT BUILDING - CONTINUOUS 42 42
scenes.summary: Joker runs to Sophie's apartment instead of his own, knocks on her door, invites her to his comedy show, and when he notices she's been crying, they kiss.

scenes.heading: 43 INT. DEPT. OF HEALTH, OFFICE - MORNING 43 43
scenes.summary: Joker meets with his social worker who is annoyed he didn't bring his journal, and he admits he lied about it not bothering him because it contains personal thoughts and original comedy material.

scenes.heading: 44 INT. MOM'S APARTMENT, KITCHEN - MORNING 44 44
scenes.summary: Joker crushes pills and sprinkles them on his mother's oatmeal, takes some pills himself while the orange cat watches, then his mother calls him to watch Thomas Wayne on TV.

scenes.heading: 45 INT. MOM'S BEDROOM, APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS 45 45
scenes.summary: Joker and his mother watch Thomas Wayne on TV discussing the subway murders, calling the victims "family" and condemning the killer as a coward while protesters spray-paint "Kill the Rich" around the city.

scenes.heading: 46 INT. COMEDY CLUB, BACKSTAGE HALLWAY - NIGHT 46 46
scenes.summary: Joker walks down the backstage hallway toward the stage in slow motion, sees Sophie take a seat, retreats into the shadows, and bangs his head against the wall before hearing the emcee introduce him.

scenes.heading: 47 EXT. COMEDY CLUB, CHINATOWN STREET - NIGHT 47 47
scenes.summary: Joker and Sophie walk out of the comedy club after his disastrous set with the audience laughing at him behind his back, and she tries to be polite about his performance while he admits he had no other friends coming.

scenes.heading: 48 INT. SZECHUAN ACE RESTAURANT, TABLE - NIGHT 48 48
scenes.summary: Joker and Sophie eat Chinese food and debate politics, with Sophie criticizing the wealthy and Thomas Wayne while Joker defends Wayne as a hero, leading to an awkward tension between them.

scenes.heading: 49 INT. MOM'S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - NIGHT 49 49
scenes.summary: Joker returns home to find his mother passed out in front of the TV playing Murray Franklin's show, checks if she's breathing, then watches Murray's sign-off and dances alone to the closing music.

scenes.heading: 50 INT. MOM'S BEDROOM, APARTMENT - EARLY MORNING 50 50
scenes.summary: Joker sits awake all night in his mother's room holding her letter, then shrieks loudly to wake her and confronts her about why she never told him Thomas Wayne is his father.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joker discovers the claim that Thomas Wayne is his father — a revelation that reframes his identity and relationship to Gotham's most powerful figure.

scenes.heading: 51 EXT. BOARDWALK, AMUSEMENT MILE - MORNING 51 51
scenes.summary: Joker walks down the boardwalk toward Ha-Ha's with a bounce in his step, passing the amusement park structures.

scenes.heading: 52 INT. HA-HA'S TALENT BOOKING, LOCKER ROOM - MORNING 52 52
scenes.summary: Joker enters the locker room where Randall and coworkers offer condolences about his firing, but Joker silently cleans out his locker while they mock him behind his back about bringing a gun to the children's hospital.

scenes.heading: 53 EXT. HA-HA'S, BACK ALLEY - AMUSEMENT MILE BOARDWALK - MORNING 53 53
scenes.summary: Randall chases Joker down the alley and confronts him about why Joker told their boss the gun was Randall's idea, while Randall's red nose falls off unnoticed.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. METRO TRAIN (MOVING) - COUNTRYSIDE, OUTSIDE GOTHAM - 54 54
scenes.summary: On a metro train, Joker stares at a tabloid headline about the killer clown and examines a photograph of Thomas Wayne taped in his notebook, parting his hair to resemble Wayne's.

scenes.heading: 55 INT. TAXI CAB (MOVING), COUNTRYSIDE - AFTERNOON 55 55
scenes.summary: Joker rides in a taxi through the countryside, staring out at the clean landscape bathed in golden light.

scenes.heading: 56 EXT. WAYNE MANOR, FRONT LAWN - MAGIC HOUR 56 56
scenes.summary: Joker walks along the fence of Wayne Manor and uses a magic wand to perform tricks for an eight-year-old boy hiding in the trees, making the boy laugh before an older woman pulls the child away and Joker is left alone at the gate.

scenes.heading: 57 EXT. LOWER EAST SIDE, STREETS - NIGHT 57 57
scenes.summary: Joker returns to his neighborhood and sees an ambulance parked in front of his building, then runs toward it with dread.

scenes.heading: 58 EXT. STREET, APARTMENT BUILDING - NIGHT 58 58
scenes.summary: Joker pushes through a crowd and reaches his unconscious mother being wheeled on a stretcher by paramedics, with two detectives following behind.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. CITY AMBULANCE, BACK (PARKED) - NIGHT 59 59
scenes.summary: Joker looks out the ambulance back doors as they close and glimpses Sophie coming out of the building while two detectives approach her.

scenes.heading: 60 INT. CITY HOSPITAL, EMERGENCY TREATMENT ROOM - NIGHT 60 60
scenes.summary: Joker stands in the doorway of an overcrowded ER treatment room watching medical staff intubate his mother, then turns away gagging.

scenes.heading: 61 EXT. CITY HOSPITAL, ER - NIGHT 61 61
scenes.summary: Two detectives question Joker outside the ER about the subway murders, asking if he knows why someone would kill three young men, while Joker denies knowledge and blames them for his mother's collapse.

scenes.heading: 62 INT. HALLWAY, NURSE'S STATION - CITY HOSPITAL - NIGHT 62 62
scenes.summary: Joker tries to check his mother out of the hospital but an ER resident tells him she had a stroke and must stay at least a week, then mentions she'll be transferred to a psych ward due to her history.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. PATIENT BAY, EMERGENCY TREATMENT ROOM - NIGHT 63 63
scenes.summary: Joker lies in a hospital bed next to his unconscious mother, laughing while watching Murray Franklin's show mock a failed stand-up comedian's videotape.
scenes.turningPointNote: Murray Franklin publicly mocks amateur comedy footage on his show, establishing the public humiliation motif that will later directly involve Joker.

scenes.heading: 64 INT. CAFETERIA, CITY HOSPITAL - EARLY MORNING 64 64
scenes.summary: Joker sits alone in the hospital cafeteria reading a tabloid with Thomas Wayne's campaign announcement on the front page.

scenes.heading: 65 EXT. CENTER FOR PERFORMING ARTS, WAYNE HALL - UPTOWN - DUSK 65 65
scenes.summary: Joker crosses the street toward Wayne Hall and sees protesters in clown masks shouting behind barricades in front of the building as it starts to rain.

scenes.heading: 66 EXT. WAYNE HALL, FRONT ENTRANCE - EVENING 66 66
scenes.summary: A fight breaks out between a masked protester and police, and the crowd pushes through barricades toward the building while Joker slips inside unnoticed.

scenes.heading: 67 INT. LOBBY, WAYNE HALL - EVENING 67 67
scenes.summary: Joker walks through the empty, opulent lobby of Wayne Hall, looking up in awe at the crystal chandeliers and marble floor.

scenes.heading: 68 INT. BACK OF THEATER, WAYNE HALL - EVENING 68 68
scenes.summary: Joker moves through the shadows at the back of the auditorium searching for Thomas Wayne while Chaplin's Modern Times plays on screen.

scenes.heading: 69 INT. LOBBY, WAYNE HALL - CONTINUOUS 69 69
scenes.summary: Joker sees Thomas Wayne heading into the men's room with security guards waiting outside, then spots a broom and dustpan in the corner.

scenes.heading: 70 INT. HALLWAY, MEN'S ROOM - WAYNE HALL - SECONDS LATER 70 70
scenes.summary: Joker sweeps the hallway with his head down, moving around the security guards' feet until they step away from the bathroom door, then he heads inside.

scenes.heading: 71 INT. MEN'S ROOM, WAYNE HALL - CONTINUOUS 71 71
scenes.summary: Joker confronts Thomas Wayne in the bathroom claiming to be his son through Penny, but Wayne calls Penny delusional and says Joker was adopted, then punches Joker in the face when Joker insists Wayne had an affair with his mother.
scenes.turningPointNote: Thomas Wayne directly denies being Joker's father, calls Penny delusional, reveals Joker was adopted, and physically assaults him—shattering Joker's belief about his identity and parentage.

scenes.heading: 72 EXT. WAYNE HALL, FRONT ENTRANCE - PLAZA - NIGHT 72 72
scenes.summary: Security guards throw Joker out of Wayne Hall in front of protesters and TV cameras, and Joker performs a theatrical tumbling fall, then takes a bow as the crowd cheers.

scenes.heading: 73 INT. APARTMENT BUILDING, HALLWAY - NIGHT 73 73
scenes.summary: Joker knocks on Sophie's apartment door while soaking wet, gets no answer, then tries the unlocked door.

scenes.heading: 74 INT. SOPHIE'S APARTMENT, FRONT HALL - CONTINUOUS 74 74
scenes.summary: Joker walks into Sophie's apartment and catches a glimpse of Sophie naked with a man on the couch, and Sophie screams when she sees him.

scenes.heading: 75 INT. HALLWAY, APARTMENT BUILDING - NIGHT 75 75
scenes.summary: Joker rushes to his mother's apartment while the man from Sophie's apartment comes out half-naked yelling at him.

scenes.heading: 76 EXT. ARKHAM STATE HOSPITAL - MORNING 76 76
scenes.summary: Joker approaches the gray Arkham State Hospital and sees police and a paramedic rolling a screaming naked man on a gurney into the entrance, then follows them inside.

scenes.heading: 77 INT. ARKHAM STATE HOSPITAL - ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE - MORNING 77 77
scenes.summary: A clerk at Arkham retrieves Penny's old file from the basement, and when Joker asks to see it the clerk begins reading that Penny was delusional and her boyfriend abused young Arthur, then the clerk steps away and Joker steals the file.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joker discovers documented evidence that his mother's claims about Thomas Wayne were delusions, that he was adopted, and that he was severely abused as a child—destroying his understanding of his own history.

scenes.heading: 78 INT. HALLWAY, ARKHAM STATE HOSPITAL - MORNING 78 78
scenes.summary: Joker runs frantically down the hospital hallway with the stolen files in his hands.

scenes.heading: 79 INT. STAIRWELL, ARKHAM STATE HOSPITAL - CONTINUOUS 79 79
scenes.summary: Joker stops in a stairwell landing and reads the file while hearing his mother's voice from her psychiatric assessment claiming Arthur is Thomas Wayne's son and denying the abuse.

scenes.heading: 80 INT. EMERGENCY INTERVIEW ROOM, ARKHAM STATE HOSPITAL - DAY 80 80
scenes.summary: In flashback, young Penny sits across from Dr. Stoner with a beaten face, and he shows her adoption paperwork and forensic photographs of three-year-old Arthur's abused body while she denies everything and smiles.

scenes.heading: 81 EXT. SCHOOL PLAYGROUND, STREET - AFTERNOON 81 81
scenes.summary: Joker confronts Sophie at the school playground about their relationship, but Sophie tells him she was just being nice and has a boyfriend, then demands he leave as other mothers turn toward them.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sophie reveals their entire relationship existed only in Joker's mind—she was never his girlfriend and barely knows him—completing the collapse of his constructed reality.

scenes.heading: 82 INT. MOM'S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - NIGHT 82 82
scenes.summary: Joker paces manically around his living room banging his head and muttering to himself while news footage of clown-masked protesters plays on TV.

scenes.heading: 83 INT. MOM'S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - MORNING 83 83
scenes.summary: Joker wakes up on the couch to a phone call from Murray Franklin's show booker saying Murray played a clip of his stand-up and wants him as a guest, and Joker skeptically picks up the phone.
scenes.turningPointNote: Murray Franklin invites Joker to appear on his show, giving Joker a public platform and setting up the climactic confrontation.

scenes.heading: 84 INT. CITY HOSPITAL, HOSPITAL ROOM (SHARED) - MORNING 84 84
scenes.summary: Joker sits on his mother's hospital bed and tells her softly but with rage that he used to think his life was a tragedy but now realizes it's a comedy.

scenes.heading: 85 INT. BLUE CURTAIN, HOSPITAL ROOM (SHARED) - CONTINUOUS 85 85
scenes.summary: The camera pulls back from behind the blue curtain divider in the hospital room, leaving behind whatever Joker is doing to his mother as applause is heard.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joker commits an act against his mother behind the curtain (strongly implied to be murder), marking his complete severance from his past identity and relationships.

scenes.heading: 86 INT. MOM'S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - NIGHT 86 86
scenes.summary: Joker studies VHS tapes of Murray Franklin's show, taking notes on how guests behave, then practices walking across the room and greeting an imaginary Murray while his cat watches.

scenes.heading: 87 EXT. POTTER'S FIELD CEMETERY - DUSK 87 87
scenes.summary: Joker stands at his mother's grave in a potter's field cemetery with his face in his hands, body convulsing, while two detectives watch from their car in the distance.

scenes.heading: 88 EXT. ROADWAY, POTTER'S FIELD CEMETERY - DUSK 88 88
scenes.summary: Joker walks away from the grave toward the two detectives, tells them they shouldn't be there, and when they ask where everyone else is he says it was always just him and his mother.

scenes.heading: 89 INT. MOM'S APARTMENT, BATHROOM - NEXT AFTERNOON 89 89
scenes.summary: Joker dyes his hair green in the bathroom while dancing to loud music, then smears white grease-paint over his face.

scenes.heading: 90 INT. FRONT DOOR, MOM'S APARTMENT - AFTERNOON 90 90
scenes.summary: Randall and Gary visit Joker's apartment to offer condolences, and Joker lets them in while palming scissors in his hand as Randall mentions the rally at City Hall.

scenes.heading: 91 INT. MOM'S BEDROOM, APARTMENT - LATE AFTERNOON 91 91
scenes.summary: Joker picks up his notebook and pauses at the photo of Bruce Wayne, then stops at the entry reading "I just hope my death make more sense than my life."

scenes.heading: 92 INT. LIVING ROOM, MOM'S APARTMENT - LATE AFTERNOON 92 92
scenes.summary: Joker takes Randall's wallet from blood-soaked pants and pockets the cash, then reaches for a gun sticking out of Randall's waistband.
scenes.turningPointNote: The scene reveals Joker has killed Randall (evidenced by blood-soaked pants and wallet-taking), marking another murder and his acquisition of a gun for the finale.

scenes.heading: 93 INT. KITCHEN, MOM'S APARTMENT - LATE AFTERNOON 93 93
scenes.summary: Joker writes a note saying "On Murray Franklin Tonight -- Please Watch" and stuffs it with Randall's money into an envelope addressed to Sophie.

scenes.heading: 94 INT. LIVING ROOM WINDOW, MOM'S APARTMENT - LATE AFTERNOON 94 94
scenes.summary: Joker opens the window and shoos his orange cat out onto the fire escape, telling it it's free, and the cat looks back at him before leaving.

scenes.heading: 95 INT. HALLWAY, APARTMENT BUILDING - LATE AFTERNOON 95 95
scenes.summary: Joker walks down the hallway in slow motion with slicked-back green hair wearing his rust-colored suit, leaves an envelope at Sophie's door along with his wand of flowers, then walks away.

scenes.heading: 96 INT. ELEVATOR, HALLWAY - LATE AFTERNOON 96 96
scenes.summary: Joker steps onto the elevator and turns to face the closing doors, revealing his full clown makeup with white grease paint, red nose, blue eye peaks, and crudely outlined red smile.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joker's complete physical transformation is revealed—he has fully assumed his new identity and appearance before heading to the Murray Franklin show.

scenes.heading: 97 EXT. APARTMENT BUILDING - LATE AFTERNOON 97 97
scenes.summary: Joker exits his building and encounters detectives Garrity and Burke, who ask where he's going, and Joker takes off running while Garrity chases on foot and Burke gets back in the car.

scenes.heading: 98 EXT. 174TH STREET, TWO LANES - CONTINUOUS 98 98
scenes.summary: Joker runs into traffic and is hit by a yellow cab, crashes into the windshield, bounces over the car and lands on the pavement, causing a multi-car pileup, then gets up and keeps running.

scenes.heading: 99 EXT. ELEVATED TRAIN STATION, STEPS - CONTINUOUS 99 99
scenes.summary: Joker hustles up the elevated train station stairs with his white grease-paint running down his face, reaches the top, and sees Garrity and Burke at the bottom.

scenes.heading: 100 EXT. PLATFORM, ELEVATED TRAIN STATION - CONTINUOUS 100 100
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: 101 INT. LEXINGTON AVE/PELHAM EXPRESS (MOVING) - CONTINUOUS 101 101
scenes.summary: Joker moves through a packed subway train filled with clown-masked protesters as detectives Burke and Garrity identify themselves as cops and push through the crowd searching for him among the many similar-looking faces.

scenes.heading: 102 EXT. WGCTV STUDIOS, FRANKLIN THEATER - MIDTOWN - EVENING 102 102
scenes.summary: An excited line of ticket holders waits outside the Franklin Theater to attend the live taping of Murray Franklin's show.

scenes.heading: 103 EXT. BACKSTAGE DOOR, FRANKLIN THEATER - SIDE STREET - EVENING 103 103
scenes.summary: Fans and autograph seekers wait by the backstage door of the Franklin Theater.

scenes.heading: 104 INT. STUDIO 4B, STAGE - FRANKLIN THEATER - EVENING 104 104
scenes.summary: The studio audience files into Studio 4B and takes their seats while the set for Murray Franklin's show remains dark.

scenes.heading: 105 INT. HALLWAY, FRANKLIN THEATER - EVENING 105 105
scenes.summary: Murray Franklin walks down the hallway in a sour mood while producer Gene Ufland expresses concern about putting Joker on the show due to the rioting outside.

scenes.heading: 106 INT. DRESSING ROOM, FRANKLIN THEATER - CONTINUOUS 106 106
scenes.summary: Murray and Gene enter the dressing room where Joker waits in his clown makeup watching riot footage on TV; Murray agrees to let him come on as 'Joker' and tells him to have fun with it.

scenes.heading: 107 INT. BACKSTAGE, BEHIND CURTAIN - STUDIO 4B - NIGHT 107 107
scenes.summary: Joker waits backstage behind the curtain watching Murray finish his interview with Dr. Sally Friedman, who jokes about whether Joker has sexual problems.

scenes.heading: 108 INT. DIRECTOR'S BOOTH, STUDIO 4B - NIGHT 108 108
scenes.summary: The director's booth prepares to come back from commercial break and cues up the clip of Joker's failed stand-up routine to play before his entrance.

scenes.heading: 109 INT. TALK SHOW SET, STAGE - STUDIO 4B - CONTINUOUS 109 109
scenes.summary: Murray introduces Joker on air, but when the curtain opens Joker doesn't come out immediately, creating an awkward pause before he finally emerges dancing to the band's music.

scenes.heading: 110 INT. DIRECTOR'S BOOTH, STUDIO 4B - CONTINUOUS 110 110
scenes.summary: The director and technical crew watch nervously from the booth, with the technical director suggesting they kill the segment.

scenes.heading: 111 INT. TALK SHOW SET, STAGE - CONTINUOUS 111 111
scenes.summary: Joker suffers a laughing fit on air, then pulls out his notebook and tells a dark joke about a man dying and going to heaven, which gets some laughs from the audience.

scenes.heading: 112 INT. DIRECTOR'S BOOTH, STUDIO 4B - CONTINUOUS 112 112
scenes.summary: The associate producer laughs at Joker's joke despite herself while the director shakes his head; on the monitors, Joker leans over and whispers something to Murray.

scenes.heading: 113 INT. TALK SHOW SET, STAGE - STUDIO 4B - CONTINUOUS 113 113
scenes.summary: Joker tells Murray his mother is dead and reveals he was adopted and sexually assaulted by his mother's boyfriend, statements the audience initially takes as dark humor.

scenes.heading: 114 INT. DIRECTOR'S BOOTH, STUDIO 4B - CONTINUOUS 114 114
scenes.summary: The director and crew in the booth realize Joker has just confessed on live television to killing the three Wall Street men, and the director orders Camera Three to zoom in close on Joker's face.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joker publicly confesses to the subway murders on live television, transforming the interview into a criminal confession and escalating the stakes irreversibly.

scenes.heading: 115 INT. TALK SHOW SET, STAGE - CONTINUOUS 115 115
scenes.summary: Murray asks Joker why they should believe his confession; Joker says he has nothing left to lose and declares his life is nothing but a comedy.

scenes.heading: 116 INT. SOPHIE'S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS 116 116
scenes.summary: Sophie watches the interview on TV with sleeping GiGi beside her as Joker tells Murray that comedy is subjective and everyone decides what's right or wrong the same way they decide what's funny or not.

scenes.heading: 117 INT. TALK SHOW SET, STAGE - STUDIO 4B - CONTINUOUS 117 117
scenes.summary: Murray tries to understand Joker's motives, but Joker says he killed those men because they were awful and criticizes Murray for crying about Thomas Wayne on TV.

scenes.heading: 118 INT. STATION SQUAD ROOM - 7TH PRECINCT - CONTINUOUS 118 118
scenes.summary: Officers and detectives at the 7th Precinct jump into action after hearing Joker confess to the Wall Street murders on live TV.

scenes.heading: 119 INT. TALK SHOW SET, STAGE - STUDIO 4B - CONTINUOUS 119 119
scenes.summary: Joker tells Murray he's awful for inviting him on just to make fun of him, and when Murray defends himself, Joker pulls out a revolver and shoots Murray in the head on live television.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joker murders Murray Franklin on live television in front of the studio audience and home viewers, committing an irreversible act of violence that completes his transformation and ignites the city.

scenes.heading: 120 INT. SOPHIE'S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS 120 120
scenes.summary: Sophie screams and jumps up horrified, waking GiGi, as Joker walks up to the camera covered in blood and screams Murray's signature sign-off before the broadcast cuts to a test pattern.

scenes.heading: 121 INT. TALK SHOW SET, STAGE - CONTINUOUS 121 121
scenes.summary: Joker is tackled by security guards and stagehands in the studio as the audience screams in terror, and his face hits the floor.

scenes.heading: 122 INT. GOTHAM SQUAD CAR (MOVING), GOTHAM STREETS - NIGHT 122 122
scenes.summary: Joker sits handcuffed in the back of a squad car gazing out at the city's violence and fires reflected in the window as the car speeds through rioting streets.

scenes.heading: 123 EXT. SQUAD CAR (SLIDING), STREET - CONTINUOUS 123 123
scenes.summary: Joker kicks open the back door of the wrecked upside-down squad car and falls out onto the street with one hand free, then leans back against the car as a crowd of rioters in Joker masks surrounds him and lifts him onto their shoulders.
scenes.turningPointNote: The rioters rescue Joker from police custody and elevate him as their symbol, cementing his transformation from isolated individual to unwitting revolutionary icon.

scenes.heading: 124 EXT. MOVIE THEATER, STREET - UPTOWN - CONTINUOUS 124 124
scenes.summary: A well-dressed crowd exits a movie theater into chaotic streets filled with violence as a silhouetted couple and their child duck into an alley, followed by a punk in a Joker mask pulling a gun.

scenes.heading: 125 EXT. SQUAD CAR (UPSIDE DOWN), STREET - CONTINUOUS 125 125
scenes.summary: Joker uses a jagged shard of glass to cut deep into both corners of his mouth, widening his smile as blood pours down his face, then leans back against the squad car and closes his eyes.

scenes.heading: 126 EXT. ALLEY, MOVIE THEATER - CONTINUOUS 126 126
scenes.summary: The punk in the Joker mask shoots both the man and woman in the alley, revealing their young son Bruce Wayne standing behind them covered in his parents' blood.
scenes.turningPointNote: Thomas and Martha Wayne are murdered in front of their son Bruce, creating Batman's origin story as a direct consequence of the chaos Joker has unleashed.

scenes.heading: 127 INT. ARKHAM STATE HOSPITAL, INTERVIEW ROOM - MORNING 127 127
scenes.summary: Joker sits in an Arkham State Hospital interview room with his head shaved, medicated, and his smile stitched up; he laughs uncontrollably then tells the doctor he just thought of a funny joke but she wouldn't get it.

scenes.heading: 128 INT. HALLWAY, ARKHAM STATE HOSPITAL - MORNING 128 128
scenes.summary: Joker shuffles down the Arkham hallway with an orderly, then begins dancing and spinning to music as he moves toward the sunset.
